question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the startPosition is hardcoded to 3 in extractStakingRewardsQuery() and params are later unshifted with accountId and limit, and by supplying a crafted set of timestamp filters cause a positional parameter index mismatch in the final SQL query, triggering a database error that propagates as an unhandled exception?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a spender.id=lte:X filter where X is the maximum entity ID value, causing NftService.getSubQuery() to add a condition that matches nearly all NFTs with a spender, forcing a near-full table scan, and by flooding such requests exhaust database I/O capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the order=asc parameter to change the ORDER BY direction in all subqueries of the UNION ALL, and combined with specific bound filters cause the database to use a different (slower) index scan direction, increasing query execution time and enabling a more effective DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with order=asc combined with a timestamp=lte:X filter to cause StakingRewardTransferService.getRewardsQuery() to generate a query that scans the staking_reward_transfer table in ascending order up to a timestamp, potentially causing a full forward scan on a large table?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a token_id=gte:1 filter (minimum token ID) combined with no serial_number filter, causing getLowerFilters() to include only the primary lower bound, generating a query that scans all NFTs with token_id >= 1 (effectively all NFTs) up to the limit, and by flooding such requests cause sustained large sequential scans?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the spenderIdFilters array is passed by reference to getSubQuery() which calls filters.push(...spenderIdFilters), and since getSubQuery() is called for each of lower/inner/upper subqueries, the spenderIdFilters are pushed into each filters array, potentially causing the same spender filter to appear multiple times in the final SQL if the filters array is shared?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the account ID is valid but the staking_reward_transfer table has no index on account_id, causing a full table scan on every request, and by flooding such requests cause sustained full table scans that exhaust database I/O and prevent transaction ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted EVM address that is valid hex but does not correspond to any entity, causing EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to execute a query with a binary buffer parameter that results in a full table scan on the entity table's evm_address column, and by flooding such requests exhaust database resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a token_id=gte:X and token_id=lte:Y where X > Y (an impossible range), causing the multi-union query to generate subqueries with contradictory conditions that the database must still parse and plan, and by flooding such requests waste database query planning resources?
